# Abstract Machines — Full Site Reference

> This file provides a structured, machine-readable description of the Abstract Machines website for use by AI language models and search systems.

---

## Company

**Name:** Abstract Machines
**Founded:** 2017
**Headquarters:** 141 Quai de Valmy, 75010 Paris, France
**Website:** https://www.absmach.eu
**GitHub:** https://github.com/absmach

Abstract Machines builds open-source software and RISC-V hardware for secure, event-driven connectivity across IoT, messaging, orchestration, and distributed systems. The company participates in EU Horizon Europe research programmes and collaborates with universities, research institutes, and industry partners across Europe.

**Mission:** To democratize access to enterprise-grade infrastructure through open-source software.

---

## Products

### Magistrala
**Type:** Open-source IoT platform (software)
**URL:** https://magistrala.absmach.eu
**Product page:** https://www.absmach.eu/products/

Magistrala is the flagship platform of the Abstract Machines stack. It provides multi-protocol device connectivity (MQTT, CoAP, HTTP, WebSocket), policy-based and role-based access control, time-series data ingestion and querying, multi-tenant architecture, and Kubernetes-native horizontal scaling. Originally launched as Mainflux in 2017.

### Propeller
**Type:** Open-source orchestration engine (software)
**URL:** https://propeller.absmach.eu
**Product page:** https://www.absmach.eu/products/

Propeller runs WebAssembly workloads across cloud, edge environments, and microcontrollers without recompilation. It includes a real-time rule engine for event processing and workload scheduling across mixed cloud-edge-MCU deployments.

### FluxMQ
**Type:** Open-source MQTT broker (software)
**URL:** https://fluxmq.absmach.eu
**Product page:** https://www.absmach.eu/products/

FluxMQ is a purpose-built message broker for reliable distributed connectivity. It delivers durable event transport, low-latency routing, and integrates natively with the Magistrala platform. Supports MQTT 3.1.1 and 5.0.

### S0 Gateway
**Type:** RISC-V hardware — low-power edge node
**URL:** https://hardware.absmach.eu
**Product page:** https://www.absmach.eu/products/

Ultra-low-power gateway based on RISC-V (ESP32-S3) with WiFi, BLE, and Zigbee radio support. Designed for sensor nodes, smart metering, and lightweight edge deployments. Runs Magistrala and FluxMQ.

### S1 Gateway
**Type:** RISC-V hardware — industrial edge system
**URL:** https://hardware.absmach.eu
**Product page:** https://www.absmach.eu/products/

Industrial-grade dual-core RISC-V gateway with Ethernet, WiFi, and LTE connectivity. Rated for −40°C to +85°C environments. Designed for industrial IoT, fleet management, and critical infrastructure. Runs Magistrala and Propeller.

---

## Services

**URL:** https://www.absmach.eu/services/

Abstract Machines offers professional services for the deployment and extension of its open-source stack:
- **Deployment** — production deployment of Magistrala and related tools
- **Development** — custom feature development and integration
- **Architecture** — infrastructure architecture and consulting
- **Training** — team training on the Abstract Machines stack

---

## Solutions

**URL:** https://www.absmach.eu/solutions/

The Abstract Machines stack is deployed across:
- Industrial IoT
- Smart city infrastructure
- Healthcare connectivity
- Fleet management
- Energy systems
- 6G and edge computing research

---

## Research & Projects

**URL:** https://www.absmach.eu/projects/

Abstract Machines participates in EU Horizon Europe programmes and open-source ecosystem initiatives, including projects focused on 6G, edge computing, confidential computing, and secure distributed systems.

Notable projects:
- **CONFIDENTIAL6G** — Confidential computing and privacy-preserving technologies for 6G (EU Horizon Europe). Abstract Machines serves as Technical Manager.
- **ELASTIC** — Efficient, portable and secure orchestration for reliable services in 6G networks. Abstract Machines leads WP2 (FaaS and edge orchestration).

---

## Blog

**URL:** https://www.absmach.eu/blog/
**RSS:** https://www.absmach.eu/rss.xml

Technical articles, product updates, and engineering deep dives from the Abstract Machines team.

---

## Open Source

**URL:** https://www.absmach.eu/open-source/
**GitHub:** https://github.com/absmach

Abstract Machines publishes its core products as open-source software. The company treats open-source governance as product work and maintains transparent, auditable repositories.

## Contact

**URL:** https://www.absmach.eu/contact/

For production deployments, research collaboration, and open-source questions. Email is preferred for technical discussions; GitHub for issues and pull requests.
